reference: Tokenization splits raw text into smaller pieces called tokens so the model can process them. In practice, token counts affect prompting, context limits, cost, and debugging.

instruction: Explain LoRA for an ML engineer new to LLM fine-tuning.
reference: LoRA adds small trainable adapter matrices instead of updating every model weight. It is popular because it reduces memory cost and makes fine-tuning easier to iterate on.

input: Focus on inference behavior in 2 sentences.
reference: KV cache reuses past attention keys and values during autoregressive generation instead of recomputing them. It improves inference latency for longer outputs without changing what the model knows.
Prompt formatting changes the token pattern the model sees. Small formatting changes can affect instruction following, output style, and evaluation results.

instruction: What is one simple hallucination check I can run offline?
reference: Compare the answer against a trusted reference and flag places where the model adds unsupported claims. This is only a heuristic, but it is useful for quick manual review.
